tei:head.tei:note: Schenker’s diary for October 3, 1922 records: “Bamberger zur 1. Stunde; nimmt nur zwei à 12 Franken, vorläufig; bittet außerdem, am 1. u 15. zahlen zu dürfen; [...]” (“Bamberger to his first lesson; takes only two lessons a week at 12 francs per lesson, preliminarily; also asks for permission to pay on the 1st and the 15th [...]”). He added a third from November 2, and later talked about adding a fourth.

tei:div.tei:ab:
Generalbass zuende. Brahms-Haydn-VariationenAnalyzed by Schenker in Der Tonwille issue 8–9, 3–46. theoretisch wiederholt u. vierhändig gespielt; auch dirigirt er Weisses u. Müllers Vierhändigspiel. Haydn: Schöpfung Chaos. Brahms: 3. Sin-
fonie.Schenker’s diary for April 22, 1923 records: “An Brahms III. Sinfonie spät in den Abend hinein für Weisse u. Bamberger.” (“Brahms' Third Symphony worked on late into the evening for Weisse and Bamberger.”). (Graphs of all four movements, undated, are preserved as OC 34/269–278.) Mozart: Sinfonie Gmoll. Bach: Brandenburgisches Konzert.Probably No. 5, BWV 1050, D major. — Schenker’s diary for May 18, 1923 records: “Bamberger tritt schon um 520h ein mit den Worten: „Ich habe etwas zu fragen“ – Gegenstand: Bogen aus dem Brandenburgischen Konzert, dann wegen des Konzertes Clemens Kraus, über die Sinfonie Gmoll.” (“Bamberger enters already at 5:20 saying: "I have something to ask." – Topic: slurs from the Brandenburg Concerto, then regarding the Clemens Kraus concert, about the Symphony in G minor.”). Versucht einen Continuo zu machen. Ich selbst mache einen. Aufführung bei Bamberger unter seiner Leitung. S. Bach: Violinkonzert, Linie u. Continuo. Für Begleitungszwecke: Schubert: Erlkönig, Die Post.“Die Post”: No. 13 of Winterreise. Gespielt: Beethoven op. 109.Schenker’s diary for October 18, 1922 records: “In die Urlinie-Mappe eingetragen: S. Bach, Violin Konzert, Adagio, u. Beethoven Appassionata, 1. Satz (Elias), op. 109 (Müller u. Bamberger), Brahms III. (Weisse) u. Streichquartett Cismoll u. Fdur (Weisse).” (“Filed in the Urlinie folder: J. S. Bach Violin Concerto, Adagio, and Beethoven Appassionata, first movement (Elias), Op. 109 (Müller and Bamberger), Brahms' Third (Weisse) and String Quartets in C minor and F major (Weisse).”). Mozart: Trio Edur K. 542. Vierhändig gespielt: Beethoven, Streichquartett Cism, Esdur.

Thoroughbass study at an end. Brahms Haydn Variations revisited from the theoretical viewpoint and played four-hands; he also conducts Weisse’s and Müller’s four-hands performance. Haydn‘s Creation, “Chaos.” Brahms
Symphony No. 3. Mozart G minor Symphony. J. S. Bach, Brandenburg Concerto. He tries to realize a continuo part. I realize one myself. Performance with Bamberger directing. J. S. Bach: Violin Concerto, Line and continuo. For purposes of accompaniment: Schubert: “Erlkönig,” “Die Post.” Played: Beethoven Op. 109, Mozart: Trio in E major K. 542. Played four-hands: Beethoven, String Quartet in C minor, E major.

tei:head.tei:note: Schenker’s diary offers little insight into the contents of Brünauer’s lessons. On November 24, 1922, however, Schenker recorded, in a significant entry: “Brünauer […] fragt plötzlich nach Urlinien in Chopins Polonaise Cismoll; zeige ihm Präludium von Bach.” (“Brünauer [...] suddenly asks for Urlinien of Chopin's Polonaise in C minor; I show him Prelude by Bach.”).

tei:div.tei:ab: Haydn: Sonate Esdur.Analyzed by Schenker in Der Tonwille issue 3, 3–21. Cf. Elias’s lesson notes. Brahms: op 10 vier Balladen, Beethoven: op. 109,Schenker’s diary for April 5, 1923 records: “Brünauer bricht in Tränen aus! HS:nach Weggang der zweiten Frau. Wählt op. 109 zur Selbstzucht im allergenauesten Vortrag; halte ihm ganz offen Geldschmutz vor – er gesteht diese Schwäche auch zu!” (“Brünauer dissolves into tears! HS:after the departure of his second wife Chooses Op. 109 to discipline himself through highly precise performance; I completely openly accuse him of money-grubbing – he also admits this weakness!”). (Brünauer had informed Schenker on March 16 that his wife had left him.) — (so genau wie möglich). Haydn: Sonaten gelesen, 8–10 Stück. Dazu Versuch eines eigenen Variationen Stückes.Schenker’s diary for April 27, 1923 records: “Brünauer bringt Skizze weiterer Variationen u. spielt.” (“Brünauer brings sketch of additional variations, and plays.”).
Haydn Sonata in E major. Brahms Four Ballades Op. 10, Beethoven Op. 109 (as accurately as possible). Haydn sonatas read through, 8–10 items. Also an attempt at a variation piece of his own.

tei:head.tei:note: Schenker’s diary for October 2, 5, and 7, 1922 records: “Frl. Elias zur 1. Stunde; bringt kein Honorar, fragt erst, ob mir cz. Kronen „angenehm“ wären.” – “Frl. Elias erklärt, das hohe Honorar nicht durchhalten zu können; ich schlage 12 Franken in unserem Gelde vor, damit sie weiter dreimal der Woche kommen kann.” – “ Frl. Elias erklärt, sehr leicht die Hälfte, 600 cz. Kronen, zahlen zu können = etwa 9 Franken.” (“Miss Elias to the first lesson; does not bring fee, first asks whether Czech Kronen would be ‘pleasant’ for me.” – “Miss Elias explains that she cannot maintain the high fee; I suggest 12 francs in our money so that she can continue to come three times per week.” – “Miss Elias says she is very easily able to pay half, 600 Czech Kronen, = approximately 9 francs.”). — The diary contains virtually no insight into the content of Elias’s lessons.

tei:div.tei:ab:
Haydn: Sonate Esdur.Analyzed by Schenker in Der Tonwille issue 3, 3–21. Cf. Brünauer’s lesson notes. Brahms: Balladen, op. 10. Beethoven: Variationen Cmoll. Haydn: Sonaten, I. Bd der
Original-Ausgabe ganz gelesen. Wiederholt: Chopin Etudes aus op. 10: Cdur, Cismoll, Fmoll u. Fdur. Theoretisches: Linien zur Appassionata,Analyzed by Schenker in Der Tonwille issue 7, 3–33. op. 109.Schenker’s diary for October 18, 1922 records: “In die Urlinie-Mappe eingetragen: S. Bach, Violin Konzert, Adagio, u. Beethoven Appassionata, 1. Satz (Elias), op. 109 (Müller u. Bamberger), Brahms III. (Weisse) u. Streichquartett Cismoll u. Fdur (Weisse).” (“Filed in the Urlinie folder: J. S. Bach Violin Concerto, Adagio, and Beethoven Appassionata, first movement (Elias), Op. 109 (Müller and Bamberger), Brahms' Third (Weisse) and String Quartets in C minor and F major (Weisse).”). Brahms: III. Sinfonie, Brahms: Händel-VariationenAnalyzed by Schenker in Der Tonwille issue 8–9, 3–46. (für Frl. Hönig) S. Bach: Solo-Stücke für Violine oder Violoncello; Kleine Präludien Am u. Ddur. Mozart: Sinfonie Gmoll begonnen.

Haydn Sonata in E major. Brahms Ballades Op. 10. Beethoven Variations in C minor. Haydn sonatas, vol. I of the
original edition read through in its entirety. Revisited: Chopin Etudes from Op. 10: C major, C minor, F minor, and F major. Theoretical matters: Lines for the Appassionata and Op. 109. Brahms Symphony No. 3, Brahms Handel Variations (for Miss Hönig) J. S. Bach: solo pieces for violin or cello; Short Preludes in A minor and D major. Mozart G minor Symphony begun.

(zweimal im MonatSchenker’s diary does not offer any direct evidence of Kahn’s lessons with him during 1922/23, though she paid 100.000 Kronen on November 4, 1922.) Wiederholungen: Beethoven: Sonaten. Chopin: Etudes, beide Hefte. Nocturnes. Beethoven: Konzert Esdur.
(twice in the month) Revisitations: Beethoven sonatas. Chopin Etudes, both books. Nocturnes. Beethoven Concerto in E major.

Zur Fugentechnik: Brahms-Händel, Fuge;Analyzed by Schenker in Der Tonwille issue 8–9, 3–46. S. Bach: Toccata Ddur; Wohltemp. Klavier: 1. Esdur u. 1. Fisdur Fuge. Stellen aus anderen Fugen. Mendelssohn: Fugen. Corrigiert zwei Fugen,
(die dritte liegt bei mir in der Bibl.)

For purposes of fugal technique: Brahms Handel Variations, fugue; J. S. Bach Toccata in D major; Well-tempered Clavier Book I: fugue in E major and Book I: F major. Passages from other fugues. Mendelssohn fugues. Two fugues corrected
(the third one I have in my own library).
AppassionataAnalyzed by Schenker in Der Tonwille issue 7, 3–33. u. Konzert Gdur wiederholt. Neu: Beethoven Konzert Cmoll, u. Eroica-Variationen. (Abgegangen mit einer Schuld von 14 Dollar.)
Appassionata and Concerto in G major revisited. New: Beethoven Concerto in C minor, and Eroica Variations. (Went off owing $14.)

tei:head.tei:note: Müller – nephew of Friedrich Siegfried Buxbaum, cellist of the Rosé Quartet and principal cellist of the Vienna Philharmonic and State Opera – took his first lesson on October 12, 1922.

tei:div.tei:ab:
Beethoven op. 109, Linie u. Spiel.Schenker’s diary for October 18, 1922 records: “In die Urlinie-Mappe eingetragen: S. Bach, Violin Konzert, Adagio, u. Beethoven Appassionata, 1. Satz (Elias), op. 109 (Müller u. Bamberger), Brahms III. (Weisse) u. Streichquartett Cismoll u. Fdur (Weisse).” (“Filed in the Urlinie folder: J. S. Bach Violin Concerto, Adagio, and Beethoven "Appassionata, first movement (Elias), Op. 109 (Müller and Bamberger), Brahms' Third (Weisse) and String Quartets in C minor and F major (Weisse).”). – He then missed a string of lessons, returning on November 13. op. 111 begonnen. Haydn: Trio Edur, etwas genauer.Schenker’s diary for January 15, 1923 records: “Müller hat noch immer nicht gezahlt; spielt Haydn, Trio.” (“Müller still has not paid; plays Haydn trio.”). Brahms: Trio Hdur, 1. Satz begonnen. Vorgespielt: Schumann: Sonate Fis-moll. Chopin: Fantasie Fmoll; Sonate Bmoll;Schenker’s diary for March 3 and May 5, 1923 records: “Müller überrascht mich mit dem Vortrag der Chopin-Sonate Bmoll.” – “Müller überrascht mich mit Chopin: Fmoll-Fantasie!” (“Müller surprises me by his performance of the Chopin Sonata in B minor.” – “Müller surprises me with Chopin: Fantasy in F minor!”). Etude Fdur aus op. 10 u. Fdur aus op. 25, Fmoll aus op. 25. Bach: Brandenburgisches KonzertProbably No. 5, BWV 1050. Schenker’s diary for May 24, 1923 records: “Bamberger berichtet über Müllers Mißverständnisse in der Probe.” (“Bamberger reports on Müller's misunderstandings in the rehearsal.”). für die Aufführung bei Bamberger HS:genau studiert. Mein Continuo in sein Heft eingetragen. Für Dirigierzwecke Haydn: Sinfonie Ddur.Probably No. 104 (“London”). Mozart:
Sinfonie G dur moll ganz.Schenker‘s diary for June 16, 1923 records: “Müller fragt um Rat, ob er dem Theater oder Konzert sich widmen soll? Ich nehme die Gelegenheit gerne wahr, ihn zur Annahme einer Stelle zu bewegen, da ich, worin sein eigenes Geständnis mich bestärkt, zu wenig Schwung finde, der einen Kampf um höhere Ziele berechtigen könnte.” (“Müller asks for advice, whether he should dedicate himself to the theater or to concertizing? I gladly take the opportunity to motivate him to take a position, since I, bolstered by his own admission, see too little momentum to justify a fight for higher goals.”). – Schenker recommended him for a position on July 18. Müller took no lessons with Schenker in 1923/24, but resumed instruction for 1924/25.

Beethoven Op. 109, Line and playing. Op. 111 begun. Haydn Trio E major, fairly accurate. Brahms Trio in B major, first movement begun. Played in front of me: Schumann Sonata in F minor. Chopin: Fantasy in F minor; B minor Sonata; Etude in F major from Op. 10, F major from Op. 25, and F minor from Op. 25. Bach Brandenburg Concerto for the performance with Bamberger HS:studied in detail. My continuo realization entered into his notebook. For conducting purposes Haydn Symphony in D major. Mozart
G major minor Symphony in its entirety.

tei:head.tei:note: On September 30, 1922, Schenker set Mrs. Pairamall’s fee at 12 francs per lesson. However, she failed to attend on October 5, and on October 14 paid 168,000 Kronen per lesson, then on November 23 paid for that month and stated that she hoped to attend in December. On December 14 she attended her first lesson and paid for the month of December. On January 14, 1923 she again attended, but in March she was evidently ill, and that illness extended to June, during which she continued to pay her lesson fees.

Weisse’s regular lessons appear to have been weekly on Fridays – but only on evidence from Schenker’s diaries, since none of his lesson notes are dated. Weisse also visited the Schenker household on a social basis and discussed musical and theoretical issues. Distinctive is the frequency of the work “Linie” – linear analysis, or “[Ur]linie.” Interesting also is the apparent collaboration with Car Bamberger.

tei:div.tei:ab:
Brahms: III. Sinfonie, 1. Satz, nicht vollendet.Schenker’s diary for Friday October 6, 1922 records: “Weisse zur 1. Stunde: Brahms III. Sinfonie .” (“Weisse to his first lesson: Brahms' Third Symphony.”). – Schenker himself then continued to work on the Urlinie of the symphony, showing Weisse his work on November 19, and again with Bamberger on April 22, 1923. — on April 22, 1923 Schenker’s diary reports: “An Brahms III. Sinfonie spät in den Abend hinein für Weisse u. Bamberger.” (“Brahms' Third Symphony worked on late into the evening for Weisse and Bamberger.”). (Graphs of all four movements, undated, are preserved as OC 34/269–278.) Beethoven: Streichquartett Cismoll u. Bdur, vierhändig, mit Analyse.Schenker’s diary for Sunday October 15 and Wednesday October 18, 1922 records: “Weisse u. Frau 4½–½8h: letzte Streichquartette von Beethoven gespielt, sogar Linie u. Vortrag erläutert.” – “ In die Urlinie-Mappe eingetragen: […] Brahms III. (Weisse) u. Streichquartett Cismoll u. Fdur (Weisse).” (“Weisse and his wife 4:30–7:30: played Beethoven's last string quartets, even elucidated Line and performance.” – “Filed in the Urlinie folder [...] Brahms' Third (Weisse) and String Quartets in C minor and F major (Weisse).”). Brahms: Klavier-Violin-Sonate op. 100, genaue Analyse.Schenker’s diary for Friday November 24, 1922 records: “½11h Weisse, meldet die Erkrankung seiner Frau: Angina – bleibt eine Stunde; Mozart Fantasie u. Brahms Sonate Adur.” (“10:30 Weisse, reports that his wife is ill: angina – stays for an hour; Mozart's Fantasy and Brahm's Sonata in A Major.”). Fragen aus: Mozart Fantasie Cmoll; Chopin: Fantasie Fmoll. Beethoven: Streichquartett Amoll, 1. Satz (Form, Linie).Schenker’s diary for Friday December 1 and Friday December 22, 1922 records: “Weisse macht statt Brahms’ III. plötzlich Klavier-Violin-Sonate Adur!” – “Weisse; erzählt von Toch; Reger-Quartett Fismoll als Gegenbeispiel betrachtet u. Brahms Sonate fortgesetzt.” (“Instead of Brahms' Third [Symphony], Weisse suddenly plays the Violin Sonata in A Major!” – “Weisse; tells about Toch; Reger's Quartet in F minor viewed as a counter-example and Brahms' sonata continued.”) Schubert: Sonate Cmoll, 1. u. 3. Satz (Linie). S. Bach: Solostücke für Violine oder Violoncello.Schenker‘s diary for Friday April 20, 1923 records: “Weisse: S. Bachs Largo aus einer Violin-Sonate; Beethoven op. 31 No 2 noch einmal die Durchführung (wegen Frl. Michelson!).” (“Weisse: J. S. Bach's Largo from a violin sonata; again the development section of Beethoven's Op. 31, No. 2 (because of Miss Michelson!). ”). Beethoven: Sonate Gdur op. 14, 1. Satz, Durchführung (Linie). Sonate Dmoll letzter Satz, Durchführung für Michelson;Cf. lesson notes for Michelson. für sie auch Händel-Variationen.Analyzed by Schenker in Der Tonwille issue 8–9, 3–46. Reger: Streichquartett Amoll, 1. Satz, Linie.Schenker’s diary for May 18, 1923 records: “Weisse tritt schon um 1020h mit den Worten ein „Ich habe viel zu fragen“! Gegenstand: op. 111, Einleitung, Linie. – Händel-Variationen, Linie u. Durchgänge; Brahms: Cello-Sonate Emoll; Beethoven: Streichquartett Amoll; bleibt auch während des Essens bis 1h!!” (“Weisse enters already at 10:20 saying "I have a lot to ask!" Topic: Op. 111, introduction, Line. – Handel Variations, Line and passing tones; Brahms: Cello Sonata in E Minor; Beethoven: String Quartet in A minor; also stays during the meal until 1:00!!”). (Reger did write a string quartet in A minor, but could there be an error here on composer name?) Beethoven:
Klavier-Violin-Sonate Ddur, 1. S., Linie. Mendelssohn: Altdeutsches Lied (Hammer zugeschickt,See JOB 94-3, [2]. letter from Schenker to Viktor Hammer, May 25, 1922: “So bleibt mir nur übrig, die kleine „Urlinie“ zu Mendelssohns’ „Altdeutschem Lied“ Ihnen u. Ihrer „schwimmenden“ Frau durch die Post zu übersenden.” (“So now I have no choice but to send you and your "floundering" wife the small Urlinie of Mendelssohn's "Old-German Song" by mail.”)., followed by diagram and explanation. (See the Schenker Documents Online homepage for a reproduction of this document. Linie). Vieles aus Bambergers Stunden.

Brahms Symphony No. 3, first movement, unfinished. Beethoven string quartets in C minor and B major, four-hands, with analysis. Brahms Violin Sonata, Op. 100, precise analysis. Questions arising from: the Mozart Fantasy in C minor and the Chopin Fantasy in F minor. Beethoven String Quartet in A minor, first movement (form, Line). Schubert Sonata in C minor, first and third movements (Line). J. S. Bach solo pieces for violin or cello. Beethoven Sonata in G major, Op. 14, first movement development section (Line). Sonata in D minor, last movement, development section for Michelson; also for her, Handel Variations. Reger String Quartet in A minor, first movement, Line. Beethoven
Violin Sonata in D major, first movement, Line. Mendelssohn “Old-German Song” (sent to Hammer, Line). Much from Bamberger‘s lessons.
